title: Cache Performance, Scope, Integrity, and Poisoning Defense · CI/CD Control Room
summary: A delivery pairing session predicts each automation transition before producing the first working cache threat and measurement policy for cache performance, scope, integrity, and poisoning defense. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of cache threat and measurement policy while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate disposable performance caches from immutable artifacts, job outputs, external registries, and release records by trust and lifetime.
Construct keys from OS, architecture, tool, dependency lock, relevant configuration, and cache version with bounded restore fallback.
Restrict who can save or restore executable caches across fork, branch, event, workflow, and privilege boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Ci Cache Artifact Distinction
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop privileged release restores executable cache content written by a fork through a broad fallback key. Compare cold and warm time, key scope, save authority, poisoning risk, and immutable artifact separation. Competency probe: Separate disposable performance caches from immutable artifacts, job outputs, external registries, and release records by trust and lifetime.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Ci Cold Warm Performance Evidence
steps.instruction:
Append the CI/CD evidence item headed "  # Evidence: ci-cold-warm-performance-evidence-1t61o2". Tailor its cache-policy.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic workflow or release review, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run GitHub Actions, runner, Docker, registry, OIDC, Google Cloud, and deployment work later in authorized disposable transfer environments.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Ci Cold Warm Performance Evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Ci Cold Warm Performance Evidence into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Ci Cold Warm Performance Evidence, keep Ci Cold Warm Performance Evidence connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ci-cold-warm-performance-evidence-1t61o2
  - competency: ci-cold-warm-performance-evidence
    artifact: cache-policy.yaml
    decision: cold warm performance evidence
    environment: reusable workflow called by three repositories at different revisions
    fault-injection: rotate a cloud trust condition during deployment
    retained-proof: accessible redacted timeline with owner expiry and regression control
    case-key: 1t61o2
    change: |
      cache-content: npm-downloads-only
      key: os-arch-node24-lockhash-config
      restore-prefix: exact-trusted-branch
      writer: trusted-push-only
      untrusted-reader: bounded-read
      node-modules: excluded
      privileged-release-cache: disabled
      cold-run: scheduled
      measurement: queue-install-build-test-upload
    verify: compare cold warm and poisoned-key fixtures while checking writer event scope restored key and critical path
    failure: Reject cache as provenance, executable fork-writable caches, broad restore keys, node_modules reuse, or warm-only evidence.
    repair: Cache disposable inputs, bind keys and writers, isolate trust domains, retain cold runs, and measure the actual critical path.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.
